codeReview.summary: The feature adds the `use net::http` surface and most of the requested diagnostics, but it does not fully cover the contracted import error behavior. In particular, qualified imported types like `http::Request` cannot reach import-aware diagnostics at all, and file-scope alias reservation is incomplete because traits named `http` are still accepted.

issues.description: The type grammar only accepts a single `located_type_name`/generic in `ty_atom`; it never parses `package::Type` paths. As a result, a contracted case like `fn f(req: http::Request) {}` cannot produce the promised import-aware diagnostic from VAL-IMPORT-004 when `use net::http` is missing — it fails earlier as a parse error instead.

Imported aliases are not actually reserved across all top-level file-scope declarations. `collect_globals` rejects collisions for functions, structs, and enums, but `ItemTrait` is added without any collision check, and `resolve_imports` also does not check `env.traits`. `use net::http; trait http { ... }` therefore succeeds silently, violating the feature requirement that imported aliases fail explicitly on file-scope collisions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `compiler-worker` skill says to write tests first, but this worker implemented production changes before editing any tests; the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`. Either the skill guidance or the expected handoff feedback standard should be clarified so reviews can rely on it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows edits to `lib/ast.ml`, `lib/parser.mly`, and `lib/resolver.ml` immediately after invoking `compiler-worker`, while the first test-file edits (`test/test_resolver.ml`, `test/test_parser.ml`, etc.) happened later.

summary: Review failed. The commit covers most import-surface behavior, but it misses qualified-type import diagnostics (`http::Request`) and leaves a file-scope collision hole for trait declarations named after imported aliases.
